div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Paraguay (Frost)Substantially the same airgram, sent as a circular, was repeated on September 19 to diplomatic representatives in Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Cuba, the Dominican Republic, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Haiti, Honduras, Nicaragua, Uruguay, and Venezuela.
div.p: A–253. The following is from War Refugee Board. Reference is made to our earlier communications on the subject of holders of Latin American documents in enemy territory.

At the suggestion of Swiss authorities, and in order to safeguard the lives of holders of documents issued in the names of American republics in German-controlled territory, this Government is endeavoring to compile lists of such holders for transmission to the German Government. A portion of a cable dated September 14, 1944 to the Legation at Bern follows:
[Here follows paraphrase of paragraph 4 of telegram 3180, September 14, printed on page 1154].
Please discuss this matter with the government to which you are accredited with a view of obtaining its confirmation of the authenticity of the list submitted by the Legation at Bern.

In making this request, you may explain that the assurances previously given on behalf of this Government that the government to which you are accredited will not be expected to grant physical admission to the persons involved and that no objection will be raised to an examination by that government of status of such persons once
they are safe from enemy persecution, fully apply to the persons whose names will be included on the lists herein envisaged.The submission of these lists to the Swiss was subsequently generally; endorsed by the other American Republics, and Swiss authorities in turn made them the subject of specific representations to the Germans.

Should the government to which you are accredited, in addition to sending such communication, be in a position itself to compile and submit to Spain a list of holders of documents issued in its name or other persons under enemy control to whom it desires to give protection, this Government would be glad to extend to such persons the benefit of its activities intended to safeguard their lives. If desired, this Government will gladly transmit any such list to the Spanish Government.
Sent to Asunción. Copy to La Paz, Bolivia. [War Refugee Board.]
